revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24744680692|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Hinatano Jinja]][[ja:比奈多乃神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Hinatano Shrine | native_name = 比奈多乃神社 | image = Hinatano Jinja cropped GSI CCB20201-C11-25 20200616.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hinatano Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.722891|138.032268}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Takehiratori|lt=Takehiratori|fr|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|zh|建比良鳥命|qid=Q11065428|1=Takehi-Nateru}} }} {{nihongo|'''Hinatano Shrine'''|比奈多乃神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tōtōmi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11642130|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province}} ** Series ordinal: 55 ** P155: {{ill|Q135039622|ltq=Q135039622|qid=Q135039622|1=Narano Shrine (Q135039622)}} ** P156: {{ill|Ohokusuno Shrine|lt=Ohokusuno Shrine|qid=Q135039623|1=Ohokusuno Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q823988|ltq=Q823988|qid=Q823988|1=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}} * {{ill|Q1048864|ltq=Q1048864|qid=Q1048864|1=Tōtōmi Province}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.722891|138.032268}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * Hinatano Jinja cropped GSI CCB20201-C11-25 20200616.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Takehiratori|lt=Takehiratori|fr|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|zh|建比良鳥命|qid=Q11065428|1=Takehi-Nateru}} == OpenCorporates ID (P1320) == * jp/6080405004806 == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181541 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917284|ltq=Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == religion or worldview (P140) == * {{ill|Q812767|ltq=Q812767|qid=Q812767|1=Shinto}} == legal form (P1454) == * {{ill|Q7311343|ltq=Q7311343|qid=Q7311343|1=religious corporation}} == country (P17) == * Улас|ca|Japó|canews|Categoria:Japó|caquote|Japó|cbk_zam|Japón|cdo|Nĭk-buōng|ceb|Hapon|ce|Ипнойчоь|chr|ᏣᏩᏂᏏ|ch|Chapan|chy|Japan|ckb|ژاپۆن|co|Giappone|crh|Japon|csb|Japòńskô|cs|Japonsko|csnews|Kategorie:Japonsko|csquote|Japonsko|csvoyage|Japonsko|cu|Ꙗпѡнїꙗ|cv|Япони|cy|Japan|dag|Japan|da|Japan|de|Japan|denews|Kategorie:Japan|dequote|Japan|devoyage|Japan|din|Japan|diq|Japonya|dsb|Japańska|dtp|Jepun|dty|जापान|dv|ޖަޕާނު|dz|ཇ་པཱན|ee|Japan|el|Ιαπωνία|elnews|Κατηγορία:Ιαπωνία|elquote|Ιαπωνία|elvoyage|Ιαπωνία|eml|Giapòṅ|en|Japan|ennews|Category:Japan|enquote|Japan|envoyage|Japan|eo|Japanio|eonews|Kategorio:Japanujo|eoquote|Japanio|eovoyage|Japanio|es|Japón|esnews|Categoría:Japón|esquote|Japón|esvoyage|Japón|et|Jaapan|etquote|Jaapan|eu|Japonia|ext|Japón|fa|ژاپن|fanews|رده:ژاپن|faquote|ژاپن|favoyage|ژاپن|ff|Japan|fiu_vro|Jaapan|fi|Japani|finews|Luokka:Japani|fivoyage|Japani|fj|Japani|fo|Japan|frp|Japon|frr|Jaapan|fr|Japon|frnews|Catégorie:Japon|frquote|Japon|frvoyage|Japon|fur|Gjapon|fy|Japan|gag|Yaponiya|gan|日本|ga|An Bikéyah|ny|Japan|oc|Japon|olo|Japounii|om|Jaappaan|or|ଜାପାନ|os|Япон|pag|Hapon|pam|Hapon|pap|Hapon|pa|ਜਪਾਨ|pcd|Japon|pcm|Japan|pdc|Japan|pfl|Japan|pl|Japonia|plnews|Kategoria:Japonia|plquote|Japonia|plvoyage|Japonia|pms|Giapon|pnb|جاپان|pnt|Ιαπωνία|ps|جاپان|pt|Japão|ptnews|Categoria:Japão|ptquote|Japão|ptvoyage|Japão|pwn|Dripun|qu|Hapun|rm|Giapun|rmy|Japan|rn|Yapon|roa_rup|Japonia|roa_tara|Giappone|ro|Japonia|ronews|Categorie:Japonia|rovoyage|Japonia|rue|Японія|ru|Япония|runews|Категория:Япония|ruvoyage|Япония|rw|Ubuyapani|sah|Дьоппуон|sat|ᱡᱟᱯᱟᱱ|sa|जपान्|scn|Giappuni|sco|Japan|sc|Giapone|sd|جاپان|se|Jáhpan|sg|Zapöo|shn|မိူင်းၵျႃႇပၢၼ်ႇ|sh|Japan|simple|Japan|si|ජපානය|skr|جاپان|sk|Japonsko|sl|Japonska|smn|Jaapaan|sm|Iapani|sn|Japani|so|Jabaan|sq|Japonia|sqnews|Kategoria:Japoni|srn|Japan|sr|Јапан|srnews|Категорија:Јапан|srquote|Јапан|ss|IJapani|stq|Japan|st|Japane|su|Jepang|sv|Japan|svnews|Kategori:Japan|svvoyage|Japan|sw|Japani|syl|ꠎꠣꠙꠣꠘ|szl|Japůńijo|szy|Zipun|ta|யப்பான்|tanews|பகுப்பு:ஜப்பான்|tay|Gipun|tcy|ಜಪಾನ್|tdd|ᥛᥫᥒᥰ ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == native label (P1705) == * {'text': '比奈多乃神社', 'language': 'ja'} == name in kana (P1814) == * ひなたのじんじゃ == postal code (P281) == * 437-1431https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6080405004806 == Commons category (P373) == * Hinatano Jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県掛川市上土方落合837', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Hinatano Jinja}} [[JA:比奈多乃神社]] {{wikidata link|Q111269921}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name = Hinatano Shrine | Image = [[File:Hinatano Jinja cropped GSI CCB20201-C11-25 20200616.jpg|280px]]
Aerial photograph of Hinatano Shrine
(June 16, 2020) | Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kakegawa City|Q823988|qid=Q823988|1=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Kamitohata Ochiai|Q131442141|qid=Q131442141|1=Kamihijikataochiai}} 837 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takehinatori-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}“Hinatano Shrine,” March 2013. | Sacred object = | Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)|Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}
{{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Founded = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichigensha nagare-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} | Other name = Tempaku Koma Shrine | Pilgrimage status = | Annual festival = Second Saturday of October | Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Hinatano
Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Hinatano Shrine''' (ひなたのじんじゃ, {{Lang-en|Hinatano Jinja}}) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Kakegawa City|Q823988|qid=Q823988|1=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. Under the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=ancient shrine ranking system|1=UNKNOWN}}, its rank was {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)|Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It was formerly also known as '''Tempaku Koma Shrine''' (てんぱくじんじゃ, {{Lang-en|Tempaku Jinja}}). === Overview === Hinatano Shrine is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Kamitohata Ochiai|Q131442141|qid=Q131442141|1=Kamihijikataochiai}}, {{ill|lt=Kakegawa City|Q823988|qid=Q823988|1=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It enshrines {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takehinatori-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its parishioners ({{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|qid=Q125562175|1=Ujiko}}) are the residents of Hinataga-tani, a {{ill|lt=small district|Q5327509|qid=Q5327509|1=koaza}} of Kamitohata Ochiai. Takehinatori-no-Mikoto is said to be a child deity of {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} and is traditionally believed to have played an active role, together with {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|qid=Q1107286}}, in the {{ill|lt=transfer of the land|Q11621119|qid=Q11621119|1=Kuni-yuzuri}}. The shrine is identified with the shrine listed as “Hinatano-no-sha”{{ill|lt=Masamune Atsuo|Q3297160|qid=Q3297160|1=Masamune Atsuo}} (ed.), *Engishiki*, Vol. 2, Nihon Koten Zenshū Kankōkai, 1929, p. 128. in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)|Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}{{ill|lt=Ministry of Doctrine|Q4294653|qid=Q4294653|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}} (ed.), *{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|qid=Q11571092|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}*, Isobe Kōyōdō, 1925, p. 290.. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takehinatori-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === [[File:Kamihijikataochiai cropped GSI CCB20201-C11-25 20200616.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Aerial photograph of {{ill|lt=Kamitohata Ochiai|Q131442141|qid=Q131442141|1=Kamihijikataochiai}}, {{ill|lt=Kakegawa City|Q823988|qid=Q823988|1=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}} (June 16, 2020). Hinatano Shrine is located slightly north of the center of Kamitohata Ochiai, while Kegon-in, which formerly conducted the shrine’s rites, is located at the northern edge of Kamitohata Ochiai.]] The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. However, since two shrines—“Naranosha” and “Hinatano-no-sha”—are listed as {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrines)|Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} of {{ill|lt=Tōtōmi Province|Q1048864|qid=Q1048864|1=Tōtōmi Province}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikui District|1=UNKNOWN}} in Volume 9 of the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}*, compiled in 927 (the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Enchō era|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|1=Enchō}}){{ill|lt=Tōtōmi Province|Q1048864|qid=Q1048864|1=Tōtōmi Province}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikui District|1=UNKNOWN}} came to be written as {{ill|lt=Jōtō District|Q11426221|qid=Q11426221|1=Kitō district}} from the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}} onward, and from the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|qid=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} onward was written exclusively as Jōtō District., the founding of Hinatano Shrine is presumed to predate 927. The *Engishiki* is a legal code of the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, compiled by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Tokihira|Q2994735|qid=Q2994735|1=Fujiwara no Tokihira}} and {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Tadahira|Q1397601|qid=Q1397601|1=Fujiwara no Tadahira}} under the order of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|qid=Q329753|1=Emperor Daigo}}. The lists of shrines recorded in Volumes 9 and 10 are known as the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, and shrines listed therein are called {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. Since its founding, the shrine has been located in Hinataga-tani of Ochiai Village, Kikui District, Tōtōmi Province{{ill|lt=Tōtōmi Province|Q1048864|qid=Q1048864|1=Tōtōmi Province}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikui District|1=UNKNOWN}} Ochiai Village later belonged to {{ill|lt=Jōtō District|Q11426221|qid=Q11426221|1=Kitō district}}.. During the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|qid=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, it came to be known as “Tempaku Koma Shrine”. With the advent of {{ill|lt=syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism|Q2279088|qid=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}, it was positioned as the {{ill|lt=guardian shrine|Q13479654|qid=Q13479654|1=Chinjusha}} of the nearby {{ill|lt=Buddhist temple|Q5393308|qid=Q5393308|1=Buddhist temple}} Kegon-in, and the shrine’s rites were thereafter conducted by Kegon-in. In the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|qid=Q3125096}}, the shrine’s name was restored to “Hinatano Shrine” in 1878 (the 11th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}). Having been ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, its rites were administered by the government until 1945 (the 20th year of {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}). === Examination concerning the Shikinaisha === === Engishiki === [[File:Engishiki cropped Engishiki Vol 2 Flame 69.jpg|thumb|200px|The section in Volume 9 of the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* included in the *Nihon Koten Zenshū* that records “Hinatano-no-sha”.]] The shrine’s origin record explicitly states that it is “one of the two shrines of Kikui District ({{ill|lt=Jōtō District|Q11426221|qid=Q11426221|1=Kitō district}}) listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō”, and Hinatano Shrine is thus regarded as a Shikinaisha recorded in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. However, because the Engishiki dates back to the Heian period, there are many Shikinaisha whose descendants became difficult to identify over time. As a result, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokugaku scholars|1=UNKNOWN}} have long pursued research to identify the descendants of Shikinaisha, and the successor of the “Hinatano-no-sha” listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō has been discussed repeatedly. === Jinmyōchō kōshō === [[File:Ban Nobutomo Zenshu cropped Ban Nobutomo Zenshu Vol 1 Frame 121.jpg|thumb|200px|The section in *Jinmyōchō Kōshō Doyo* included in *Ban Nobutomo Zenshū* that discusses “Hinatano-no-sha”{{ill|lt=Ichishima Kenkichi|Q11480125|qid=Q11480125|1=Kenkichi Ichishima}} (ed.), *Ban Nobutomo Zenshū*, Vol. 1, {{ill|lt=Kokusho Kankōkai (1905–1922)|Q28686029|qid=Q28686029|1=Kokusho Kankōkai (1905–1922)}}, 1907, p. 226..]] The Edo-period kokugaku scholar {{ill|lt=Ban Nobutomo|Q4346747|qid=Q4346747|1=Ban Nobutomo}} studied ancient shrines and authored *{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Doyo|Q11588823|qid=Q11588823|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|1=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dodai}}*. In it, he discusses the “Hinatano-no-sha” of the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, stating that its enshrined deity was “the child of {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohihi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}, {{ill|lt=Takehiratori-no-Mikoto|Q11065428|qid=Q11065428|1=Takehi-Nateru}}, {{ill|lt=Tōtōmi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|UNKNOWN|lt=Tōtōmi Province no Miyatsuko}}”. He then introduces a theory identifying it with {{ill|lt=Takaten Shrine|Q11669115|qid=Q11669115|1=Taka Tenjinja}}, located on the summit of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mount Takatenjin|1=UNKNOWN}}, south of {{ill|lt=Kakegawa-juku|Q3070229|qid=Q3070229|lt=Kakegawa Station|1=Kakegawa-juku}}, citing as evidence the presence of a mountain village called Hinataga-tani at the foot of Mount Takatenjin. At the same time, Ban also introduces the theory of the local kokugaku scholar {{ill|lt=Kurita Domaru|Q6536151|qid=Q6536151|1=Kurita Hijimaro}}, who proposed identifying the shrine with the small guardian shrine of Kegon-in located in Hinataga-tani of Hijikata District. Notably, the enshrined deities of Takaten Shrine are {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}}, Ame-no-Hohihi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=UNKNOWN}}“Takaten Shrine.”, not Takehiratori-no-Mikoto. By contrast, Hinatano Shrine in Kamitohata Ochiai enshrines Takehinatori-no-Mikoto, who is regarded as the same divine entity as Takehiratori-no-Mikoto. Furthermore, Takaten Shrine is located in {{ill|lt=Kamitohata Mineko|Q131426754|qid=Q131426754|1=Kamihijikataminemukai}}, Kakegawa City, very close to Hinatano Shrine in Kamitohata Ochiai. Kamitohata Mineko and Kamitohata Ochiai are neighboring {{ill|lt=ōaza|Q424857|qid=Q424857|1=ōaza}} that both formerly belonged to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamitohata Village|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Jōtō District|Q11426221|qid=Q11426221|1=Kitō district}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu Prefecture|Q11557878|qid=Q11557878|1=Hamamatsu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hamamatsu Prefecture|Q11557878|qid=Q11557878|1=Hamamatsu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Jōtō District|Q11426221|qid=Q11426221|1=Kitō district}} Ochiai Village merged with Mineko Village and Dantsuke Shinden to form Kamitohata Village in 1875.. However, Hinatano Shrine in Kamitohata Ochiai is the {{ill|lt=tutelary shrine|Q767655|qid=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of Hinataga-tani and is located within Hinataga-tani itself, whereas Takaten Shrine is not located in Hinataga-tani but merely in its vicinity. === Jinja Kakuroku === [[File:Jinja Kakuroku cropped Jinja Kakuroku Part Jo Frame 439.jpg|thumb|200px|The section in the upper volume of *{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|qid=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}* that discusses “Hinatano-no-sha”{{ill|lt=Suzuka Tomotane|Q6536446|qid=Q6536446|lt=Nakatomi no Asomi Tomotane|1=Suzuka Tsuratane}} (comp.), “Tōtōmi Province,” in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Suzuka Yoshikujira|1=UNKNOWN}}’s works, ed. by {{ill|lt=Inoue Yorikuni|Q6535302|qid=Q6535302|1=Inoue Yorikuni}} and {{ill|lt=Saeki Ariyoshi|Q11382847|qid=Q11382847|1=Saeki Ariyoshi}}, *Jinja Kakuroku*, Upper Volume, {{ill|lt=Kōten Kōkyūjo|Q11580997|qid=Q11580997|1=Koten Kokyusho}}, 1902, p. 857..]] Another Edo-period Shinto priest, {{ill|lt=Suzuka Tomotane|Q6536446|qid=Q6536446|1=Suzuka Tsuratane}}, also researched ancient shrines and authored *{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|qid=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}*. In it, he states regarding the “Hinatano-no-sha” of the Engishiki Jinmyōchō that “the enshrined deity and location are unclear”, suggesting that details of its successor shrine had already become unknown. He then introduces the theory identifying it with Takaten Shrine, citing the existence of Hinataga-tani at the foot of Mount Takatenjin as evidence. He also introduces Kurita Domaru’s theory identifying it with Hinatano Shrine, the guardian shrine of Kegon-in in Hinataga-tani. === Tokusen Jinmyōchō === [[File:Tokusen Jimmyocho cropped Tokusen Jimmyocho Frame 182.jpg|thumb|200px|The section in *{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|qid=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}* that discusses “Hinatano-no-sha”.]] In the Meiji period, research into ancient shrines was also advanced by the {{ill|lt=Ministry of Doctrine|Q4294653|qid=Q4294653|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}}, one of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=central government ministries|1=UNKNOWN}}, and the results were published as the *{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|qid=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}*. Therein, the “Hinatano-no-sha” of the Engishiki Jinmyōchō is recorded as “Hinatano-no-sha, also called Tempaku Koma Shrine”, and its location is clearly specified as Hinataga-tani, Ochiai Village ({{ill|lt=Ogasawara District|Q7079825|qid=Q7079825|1=Ogasa District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Hijikata Village|Q11423527|qid=Q11423527|1=Hijikata}} Ōaza {{ill|lt=Kamitohata|Q131527787|qid=Q131527787|1=Kamihijikata}}, {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} Hinatano-no-sha){{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ogasawara District|Q7079825|qid=Q7079825|1=Ogasa District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Hijikata Village|Q11423527|qid=Q11423527|1=Hijikata}} later merged with {{ill|lt=Satsuka Village|Q11383208|qid=Q11383208|1=Sazuka}} to form {{ill|lt=Jōtō Village (Ogasawara District, Shizuoka)|Q11426205|qid=Q11426205|1=Kitō}} in 1955., explicitly identifying it with Hinatano Shrine in Kamitohata Ochiai. As supporting evidence, it cites *Jinja Kakuroku*, as well as local traditions that Ochiai Village was the original site of the Shikinaisha, and records of Kegon-in indicating that Hinatano Shrine, also known as Tempaku Koma Shrine, was a Shikinaisha and the guardian shrine at the time of Kegon-in’s founding. Based on this evidence, the *Tokusen Jinmyōchō* identifies the “Hinatano-no-sha” of the Engishiki Jinmyōchō with Hinatano Shrine in Kamitohata Ochiai. Thereafter, the successor of the “Hinatano-no-sha” of the Engishiki Jinmyōchō has generally been regarded as Hinatano Shrine in Kamitohata Ochiai. For example, the *History of Shizuoka Prefecture*, published in 1936 (Shōwa 11), states that the original location was Hinataga-tani, Hijikata Village, Ogasawara District, and that the present shrine is Hinatano Shrine at that site, making no mention of Takaten Shrine*History of Shizuoka Prefecture*, Vol. 3, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, 1936, p. 698.. === Precincts === The shrine buildings are constructed in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichigensha nagare-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} style. The surrounding trees form a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine grove|1=UNKNOWN}}. Rice paddies and tea fields spread out around the precincts. === Parishioner area === * Hinataga-tani, {{ill|lt=Kamitohata Ochiai|Q131442141|qid=Q131442141|1=Kamihijikataochiai}}, {{ill|lt=Kakegawa City|Q823988|qid=Q823988|1=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}} === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Notes === {{Reflist|2|group="†"}} === Miyatsuko}} * {{ill|lt=Hijikata Village|Q11423527|qid=Q11423527|1=Hijikata}} * {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu shūgō|Q2279088|qid=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} * {{ill|lt=Chinjugami|Q97479976|qid=Q97479976|1=Chinjugami}} * {{ill|lt=Tempaku faith|Q3616095|qid=Q3616095|1=Tenpaku faith}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4409208 Hinatano Shrine – Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines] – A page introducing Hinatano Shrine by the {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines|Q134956727|qid=Q134956727|1=Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Association}} (the location is listed as “Kakegawa City, Hinataga-tani”). {{Shinto-stub}}
